prompt_text:
I'm going to describe two scenarios. In the memory scenario, a novel object called a clop has a property: it can be activated by pressing one of its buttons. Your job is to figure out which object in the perception scenario is the clop-analog, and therefore which button on it can be activated.

Memory scenario: There are 7 objects on a table: a clop, a wren, a thorp, a vro, a mek, a dask, and a xav. The clop is underneath the wren. The wren is to-the-left-of the thorp. The thorp is above the vro. The vro is to-the-right-of the mek. The mek is underneath the dask. The dask is to-the-left-of the xav. The clop has a blue button on top, a red button on side, and a yellow button on bottom. The wren has a yellow button on top and a blue button on bottom. The thorp has a yellow button on top and a blue button on bottom. The vro has a yellow button on top and a blue button on bottom. The mek has a yellow button on top and a blue button on front. The dask has a yellow button on top and a blue button on bottom. The xav has a yellow button on top and a blue button on side. Pressing the blue button on the clop activates it.

Perception scenario: There are 7 objects on a shelf: a woz, a nurp, a greeble, a quib, a wix, a tunk, and a brall. The tunk is underneath the greeble. The greeble is to-the-left-of the woz. The woz is above the quib. The quib is to-the-right-of the wix. The wix is underneath the nurp. The nurp is to-the-left-of the brall. The woz has a yellow button on top and a yellow button on bottom. The nurp has a red button on top and a yellow button on bottom. The greeble has a red button on top, a blue button on side, and a yellow button on bottom. The quib has a red button on top and a red button on bottom. The wix has a yellow button on top and a red button on bottom. The tunk has a blue button on top, a red button on side, and a yellow button on bottom. The brall has a yellow button on top and a red button on bottom.

Which object in the perception scenario is the clop-analog, and which button activates it?
